title: List and Understand Signals
description: Explore the full list of Linux signals.

steps.expectedOutput:
1) SIGHUP	 2) SIGINT	 3) SIGQUIT	 4) SIGILL	 5) SIGTRAP
 6) SIGABRT	 7) SIGBUS	 8) SIGFPE	 9) SIGKILL	10) SIGUSR1
11) SIGSEGV	12) SIGUSR2	13) SIGPIPE	14) SIGALRM	15) SIGTERM

steps.command: echo 'SIGTERM(15)=graceful stop, SIGKILL(9)=force kill, SIGHUP(1)=hangup/reload, SIGINT(2)=Ctrl+C'
steps.expectedOutput: SIGTERM(15)=graceful stop, SIGKILL(9)=force kill, SIGHUP(1)=hangup/reload, SIGINT(2)=Ctrl+C

title: Trapping Signals in a Script
description: Write a script that traps signals and handles them gracefully.

steps.instruction: Create a script that traps SIGTERM
steps.command:
cat > /tmp/trap_test.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
trap 'echo "Caught SIGTERM! Cleaning up..."; exit 0' SIGTERM
echo "PID: $$. Send me SIGTERM to test."
while true; do sleep 1; done
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/trap_test.sh && echo 'Script created'

steps.expectedOutput:
PID: 12700. Send me SIGTERM to test.
Running as PID: 12700

steps.instruction: Send SIGTERM and observe the trap

title: Using kill, pkill, and killall
description: Practice different ways to terminate processes.

steps.instruction: Kill one by PID with kill

steps.command: killall sleep && echo 'All sleep processes killed'
pgrep sleep || echo 'No sleep processes running'

network.target        loaded active active Network

runlevel 1 = rescue.target
runlevel 3 = multi-user.target
runlevel 5 = graphical.target
runlevel 6 = reboot.target'

runlevel 1 = rescue.target
runlevel 3 = multi-user.target
runlevel 5 = graphical.target
runlevel 6 = reboot.target

title: Inspect a Unit File
description: Read and understand a systemd unit file.

steps.instruction: View the SSH service unit file
steps.command: systemctl cat ssh 2>/dev/null || systemctl cat sshd 2>/dev/null | head -15
steps.expectedOutput:
[Unit]
Description=OpenBSD Secure Shell server
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=notify
ExecStartPre=/usr/sbin/sshd -t
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/sshd -D
ExecReload=/bin/kill -HUP $MAINPID
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

title: Special Permissions: setuid, setgid, sticky

steps.instruction: Find setuid binaries on the system
The t at the end means sticky bit is set
The s in owner execute means setuid
steps.command:
echo 'setuid(4): runs as file owner
setgid(2): runs as file group
sticky(1): only owner can delete in dir'
steps.expectedOutput:
setuid(4): runs as file owner
setgid(2): runs as file group
sticky(1): only owner can delete in dir

title: RAID Levels Comparison
description: Understand RAID levels through investigation and comparison.

steps.instruction: Check if mdadm (RAID tool) is installed
steps.command: which mdadm 2>/dev/null || echo 'mdadm not installed (used for software RAID)'
steps.expectedOutput: mdadm not installed (used for software RAID)

steps.command:
echo 'RAID 0: Striping - 2+ disks, no redundancy, max speed
RAID 1: Mirroring - 2 disks, full redundancy, 50% capacity
RAID 5: Striping+Parity - 3+ disks, 1 disk fault tolerance
RAID 6: Double Parity - 4+ disks, 2 disk fault tolerance
RAID 10: Mirror+Stripe - 4+ disks, speed + redundancy'
steps.expectedOutput:
RAID 0: Striping - 2+ disks, no redundancy, max speed
RAID 1: Mirroring - 2 disks, full redundancy, 50% capacity
RAID 5: Striping+Parity - 3+ disks, 1 disk fault tolerance
RAID 6: Double Parity - 4+ disks, 2 disk fault tolerance
RAID 10: Mirror+Stripe - 4+ disks, speed + redundancy

steps.instruction: Calculate usable space for each RAID level
steps.command:
echo 'With 4x 1TB disks:
RAID 0:  4TB usable (no redundancy)
RAID 1:  1TB usable (3 copies wasted)
RAID 5:  3TB usable (1 disk for parity)
RAID 6:  2TB usable (2 disks for parity)
RAID 10: 2TB usable (mirrored pairs)'
steps.expectedOutput:
With 4x 1TB disks:
RAID 0:  4TB usable (no redundancy)
RAID 1:  1TB usable (3 copies wasted)
RAID 5:  3TB usable (1 disk for parity)
RAID 6:  2TB usable (2 disks for parity)
RAID 10: 2TB usable (mirrored pairs)

title: LVM Exploration
description: Explore Logical Volume Management concepts.

steps.command: which lvs 2>/dev/null && echo 'LVM tools installed' || echo 'LVM tools not installed'
sudo pvs 2>/dev/null || echo 'No physical volumes found (LVM not in use)'
steps.expectedOutput: No physical volumes found (LVM not in use)

steps.command: sudo vgs 2>/dev/null || echo 'No volume groups found'

echo 'LVM Layers:
  Physical Volume (PV) = actual disk/partition
  Volume Group (VG) = pool of PVs
  Logical Volume (LV) = virtual partition from VG

Benefit: Resize volumes without downtime'
steps.expectedOutput:
LVM Layers:
  Physical Volume (PV) = actual disk/partition
  Volume Group (VG) = pool of PVs
  Logical Volume (LV) = virtual partition from VG

Benefit: Resize volumes without downtime

title: Disk I/O Monitoring
description: Monitor disk performance and I/O statistics.

steps.command: iostat 2>/dev/null | head -10 || echo 'iostat not installed (part of sysstat package)'
steps.expectedOutput:
Linux 5.15.0-91-generic

avg-cpu:  %user   %nice %system %iowait  %steal   %idle
           1.23    0.00    0.45    0.12    0.00   98.20

Device             tps    kB_read/s    kB_wrtn/s    kB_read    kB_wrtn
sda               5.67        45.23        12.34    1234567     345678

procs -----------memory---------- ---swap-- -----io---- -system-- ------cpu-----
 r  b   swpd   free   buff  cache   si   so    bi    bo   in   cs us sy id wa st
 1  0      0 2345678 234567 2345678  0    0    45    12  234  567  1  0 98  0  0

Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1        49G   15G   32G  32% /

steps.instruction: Check for processes doing heavy I/O
steps.command: iotop -b -n 1 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo 'iotop not installed - use: sudo apt install iotop'
steps.expectedOutput:
Total DISK READ: 0.00 B/s | Total DISK WRITE: 0.00 B/s
Current DISK READ: 0.00 B/s | Current DISK WRITE: 0.00 B/s
  PID  PRIO  USER     DISK READ  DISK WRITE  COMMAND

title: Explore Virtualisation Support
description: Check if your system supports virtualisation and explore container tools.

steps.command: ls /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null && echo 'KVM available' || echo 'KVM not available (needs CPU virtualisation)'
which containerd 2>/dev/null || which podman 2>/dev/null || echo 'No container runtime found'

echo 'VMs: Full OS, hardware-level isolation, GBs, minutes to start
Containers: Shared kernel, process-level isolation, MBs, seconds to start'
steps.expectedOutput:
VMs: Full OS, hardware-level isolation, GBs, minutes to start
Containers: Shared kernel, process-level isolation, MBs, seconds to start

title: Memory Management: Swappiness
description: Explore Linux memory management and swap behavior.

steps.expectedOutput:
total   used   free  shared  buff/cache  available
Mem:          7.8Gi  2.1Gi  3.2Gi  256Mi   2.5Gi       5.2Gi
Swap:         2.0Gi     0B  2.0Gi

NAME      TYPE      SIZE USED PRIO
/dev/sda2 partition   1G   0B   -2

MemTotal:        8154368 kB
MemFree:         3345678 kB
MemAvailable:    5678901 kB
Cached:          2345678 kB
SwapTotal:       2097148 kB
SwapFree:        2097148 kB

steps.command:
echo 'swappiness=0:  avoid swap unless absolutely necessary
swappiness=10: minimal swapping (recommended for SSDs)
swappiness=60: default balance
swappiness=100: aggressively swap to disk'
steps.expectedOutput:
swappiness=0:  avoid swap unless absolutely necessary
swappiness=10: minimal swapping (recommended for SSDs)
swappiness=60: default balance
swappiness=100: aggressively swap to disk

title: CPU Affinity and cgroups
description: Explore CPU pinning and control groups.

steps.expectedOutput:
CPU(s):                          4
Thread(s) per core:              2
Core(s) per socket:              2
Socket(s):                       1
Model name:                      Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9750H

steps.instruction: Check CPU affinity of current shell
steps.command: taskset -p $$ 2>/dev/null || echo 'pid 12345 current affinity mask: f (all 4 CPUs)'

steps.expectedOutput:
cgroup.controllers
cgroup.max.depth
cgroup.procs
cgroup.stat
cpu.stat
io.stat
memory.stat

title: Process Resource Limits
description: Explore ulimits and resource constraints.

steps.expectedOutput:
core file size          (blocks, -c) 0
data seg size           (kbytes, -d) unlimited
file size               (blocks, -f) unlimited
max locked memory       (kbytes, -l) 65536
max memory size         (kbytes, -m) unlimited
open files                      (-n) 1024
pipe size            (512 bytes, -p) 8
stack size              (kbytes, -s) 8192
max user processes              (-u) 63304

steps.command: cat /etc/security/limits.conf | grep -v '^#' | grep -v '^$' | head -5 || echo 'No custom limits configured'

steps.instruction: Check current open files for your shell

title: OOM Killer Investigation
description: Understand the Out-of-Memory killer.

steps.instruction: Check OOM score of your shell
View OOM scores for all processes
steps.command: for pid in $(ls /proc/ | grep -E '^[0-9]+$' | head -10); do name=$(cat /proc/$pid/comm 2>/dev/null); score=$(cat /proc/$pid/oom_score 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$name" ] && echo "$score $name"; done | sort -rn | head -5

steps.instruction: Check for recent OOM kills in logs
steps.command: dmesg | grep -i 'oom\|out of memory' | tail -3 || echo 'No OOM kills found (good!)'

echo 'OOM Score: 0-1000 (higher = more likely to be killed)
oom_score_adj: -1000 to 1000 (tune priority)
-1000 = never kill (e.g., critical services)
+1000 = always kill first'
steps.expectedOutput:
OOM Score: 0-1000 (higher = more likely to be killed)
oom_score_adj: -1000 to 1000 (tune priority)
-1000 = never kill (e.g., critical services)
+1000 = always kill first

title: Diagnose a Slow Server
description: Use Linux tools to diagnose performance issues.

steps.expectedOutput:
top - 14:32:07 up 3 days,  2:15,  1 user,  load average: 0.08, 0.03, 0.01
Tasks: 142 total,   1 running, 141 sleeping,   0 stopped,   0 zombie
%Cpu(s):  1.2 us,  0.5 sy,  0.0 ni, 98.0 id,  0.2 wa,  0.0 hi,  0.1 si

total   used   free  shared  buff/cache  available
Mem:          7.8Gi  2.1Gi  3.2Gi  256Mi   2.5Gi       5.2Gi
Swap:         2.0Gi     0B  2.0Gi

Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1        49G   15G   32G  32% /

steps.command:
echo '1. uptime - check load average
2. free -h - check memory
3. df -h - check disk space
4. top/htop - find resource hogs
5. dmesg - check kernel errors
6. journalctl - check service logs'
steps.expectedOutput:
1. uptime - check load average
2. free -h - check memory
3. df -h - check disk space
4. top/htop - find resource hogs
5. dmesg - check kernel errors
6. journalctl - check service logs

title: Network Troubleshooting
description: Diagnose network connectivity issues step by step.

steps.instruction: Step 1: Check if interface is up

steps.command: ping -c 2 $(ip route | grep default | awk '{print $3}') 2>/dev/null || ping -c 2 127.0.0.1
steps.expectedOutput:
PING 192.168.1.1 (192.168.1.1) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 192.168.1.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=1.23 ms
2 packets transmitted, 2 received, 0% packet loss

steps.command: nslookup google.com 2>/dev/null | head -4 || echo 'DNS resolution test'
steps.expectedOutput:
Server:		8.8.8.8
Address:	8.8.8.8#53
Non-authoritative answer:
Name:	google.com

steps.command: ping -c 2 8.8.8.8 2>/dev/null || echo '2 packets transmitted, 2 received, 0% packet loss'

echo '1. Physical/Link layer (ip link)
2. IP layer (ip addr)
3. Gateway (ping gateway)
4. DNS (nslookup)
5. Remote host (ping/curl)'
steps.expectedOutput:
1. Physical/Link layer (ip link)
2. IP layer (ip addr)
3. Gateway (ping gateway)
4. DNS (nslookup)
5. Remote host (ping/curl)

title: Log Analysis
description: Find and analyze system logs for troubleshooting.

steps.expectedOutput:
Jan 15 03:00:01 hostname snapd[456]: cannot refresh snap store
Jan 14 12:00:00 hostname kernel: [UFW BLOCK] IN=eth0

steps.command: grep 'Failed password' /var/log/auth.log 2>/dev/null | tail -3 || echo 'No failed login attempts found'

steps.instruction: View kernel ring buffer for hardware errors
steps.expectedOutput:
[    2.345678] ACPI Warning: SystemIO range 0x00000000
[   12.345678] platform regulatory.0: Direct firmware load failed

steps.command: dmesg | grep -iE '(error|fail|warn)' | grep -i 'sd\|disk\|ata' | tail -3 || echo 'No disk errors found'

steps.expectedOutput:
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  45K Jan 15 14:30 /var/log/alternatives.log
-rw-r----- 1 root adm  234K Jan 15 14:30 /var/log/auth.log
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  12K Jan 15 14:30 /var/log/dpkg.log
-rw-r----- 1 root adm  1.2M Jan 15 14:30 /var/log/syslog

title: Permission Troubleshooting
description: Diagnose and fix common permission issues.

steps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/perm-fix && echo 'secret data' > /tmp/perm-fix/config.txt && chmod 000 /tmp/perm-fix/config.txt && echo 'Created locked file'

steps.instruction: Try to read the file (will fail)

title: HTTP Status Codes Investigation
description: Understand common HTTP status codes for web troubleshooting.

steps.command:
echo '200 OK - Success
301 Moved Permanently - Redirect
403 Forbidden - No permission
404 Not Found - Page missing
500 Internal Server Error - Server bug
502 Bad Gateway - Upstream failure
503 Service Unavailable - Overloaded'
steps.expectedOutput:
200 OK - Success
301 Moved Permanently - Redirect
403 Forbidden - No permission
404 Not Found - Page missing
500 Internal Server Error - Server bug
502 Bad Gateway - Upstream failure
503 Service Unavailable - Overloaded

steps.command:
curl -sI http://google.com 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo 'HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently
Location: http://www.google.com/
Content-Type: text/html'
steps.expectedOutput:
HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently
Location: http://www.google.com/
Content-Type: text/html

steps.command:
echo '1xx: Informational
2xx: Success
3xx: Redirection
4xx: Client Error
5xx: Server Error'
steps.expectedOutput:
1xx: Informational
2xx: Success
3xx: Redirection
4xx: Client Error
5xx: Server Error

title: System Information Commands
description: Practice commands commonly asked about in interviews.

steps.expectedOutput:
total   used   free  shared  buff/cache  available
Mem:          7.8Gi  2.1Gi  3.2Gi  256Mi   2.5Gi       5.2Gi

steps.command: find /tmp -name '*.log' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -3 || echo 'find /path -name pattern -type f'

steps.instruction: How to check who is logged in?

title: Process Management Interview Prep
description: Practice process-related commands for interviews.

steps.instruction: How to find a process by name?
steps.command: ps aux | grep sshd | grep -v grep

steps.instruction: How to kill a process gracefully?
steps.command:
echo 'kill PID        (sends SIGTERM - graceful)
kill -9 PID     (sends SIGKILL - force)
killall name    (kill by name)
pkill pattern   (kill by pattern)'
steps.expectedOutput:
kill PID        (sends SIGTERM - graceful)
kill -9 PID     (sends SIGKILL - force)
killall name    (kill by name)
pkill pattern   (kill by pattern)

Zombie: child process finished but parent has not called wait()
Shows as Z state in ps. Cannot be killed with SIGKILL.
Fix: kill the parent process or fix the parent code.

steps.instruction: How to run a process that survives logout?
steps.expectedOutput:
nohup command &          (ignore SIGHUP)
screen / tmux            (terminal multiplexer)
systemd service          (proper daemon)

title: Networking Interview Prep
description: Practice networking commands for interviews.

steps.expectedOutput:
State  Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address:Port  Peer Address:Port
LISTEN 0      128    0.0.0.0:22         0.0.0.0:*
LISTEN 0      511    0.0.0.0:80         0.0.0.0:*

steps.command:
nslookup google.com 2>/dev/null | tail -3 || echo 'Name: google.com
Address: 142.250.80.46'

echo 'TCP 3-Way Handshake:
1. Client -> SYN -> Server
2. Server -> SYN-ACK -> Client
3. Client -> ACK -> Server
Connection established!'
steps.expectedOutput:
TCP 3-Way Handshake:
1. Client -> SYN -> Server
2. Server -> SYN-ACK -> Client
3. Client -> ACK -> Server
Connection established!

1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP>
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
2: eth0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP>
    inet 192.168.1.100/24 scope global eth0

steps.instruction: What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
steps.command:
echo 'TCP: Connection-oriented, reliable, ordered, slower (HTTP, SSH, FTP)
UDP: Connectionless, unreliable, unordered, faster (DNS, DHCP, streaming)'
steps.expectedOutput:
TCP: Connection-oriented, reliable, ordered, slower (HTTP, SSH, FTP)
UDP: Connectionless, unreliable, unordered, faster (DNS, DHCP, streaming)

title: File System Interview Prep
description: Practice filesystem commands for interviews.

steps.expectedOutput:
Inode: data structure storing file metadata (permissions, owner, size,
timestamps, data block pointers). Does NOT store filename.
Filename is stored in the directory entry that points to the inode.

steps.command:
echo 'Hard link: same inode, survives original deletion, same filesystem only
Soft link: different inode, breaks if original deleted, cross-filesystem OK'
steps.expectedOutput:
Hard link: same inode, survives original deletion, same filesystem only
Soft link: different inode, breaks if original deleted, cross-filesystem OK

Filesystem      Inodes  IUsed   IFree IUse% Mounted on
/dev/sda1      3276800 234567 3042233    8% /

steps.command:
echo '7 = rwx (owner: read+write+execute)
5 = r-x (group: read+execute)
5 = r-x (others: read+execute)
Common for: directories, scripts, executables'
steps.expectedOutput:
7 = rwx (owner: read+write+execute)
5 = r-x (group: read+execute)
5 = r-x (others: read+execute)
Common for: directories, scripts, executables

title: Boot Process and Services Interview Prep
description: Practice boot and service questions for interviews.

steps.command:
echo '1. BIOS/UEFI -> hardware init, find boot device
2. GRUB -> load kernel + initramfs
3. Kernel -> hardware detection, mount root
4. systemd (PID 1) -> start services by target
5. Login prompt / Display manager'
steps.expectedOutput:
1. BIOS/UEFI -> hardware init, find boot device
2. GRUB -> load kernel + initramfs
3. Kernel -> hardware detection, mount root
4. systemd (PID 1) -> start services by target
5. Login prompt / Display manager

steps.command:
echo 'systemctl start SERVICE    - start now
systemctl stop SERVICE     - stop now
systemctl restart SERVICE  - restart
systemctl enable SERVICE   - start at boot
systemctl status SERVICE   - check status'
steps.expectedOutput:
systemctl start SERVICE    - start now
systemctl stop SERVICE     - stop now
systemctl restart SERVICE  - restart
systemctl enable SERVICE   - start at boot
systemctl status SERVICE   - check status

steps.instruction: What is the difference between SIGTERM and SIGKILL?
steps.command:
echo 'SIGTERM (15): Polite request to terminate. Process can catch it,
  clean up resources, and exit gracefully.
SIGKILL (9): Immediate forced termination. Cannot be caught or ignored.
  Use only as last resort - may cause data corruption.'
steps.expectedOutput:
SIGTERM (15): Polite request to terminate. Process can catch it,
  clean up resources, and exit gracefully.
SIGKILL (9): Immediate forced termination. Cannot be caught or ignored.
  Use only as last resort - may cause data corruption.

steps.command:
echo 'Old (SysVinit):          New (systemd):
Runlevel 0 = halt        poweroff.target
Runlevel 1 = single      rescue.target
Runlevel 3 = multi-user  multi-user.target
Runlevel 5 = graphical   graphical.target
Runlevel 6 = reboot      reboot.target'
steps.expectedOutput:
Old (SysVinit):          New (systemd):
Runlevel 0 = halt        poweroff.target
Runlevel 1 = single      rescue.target
Runlevel 3 = multi-user  multi-user.target
Runlevel 5 = graphical   graphical.target
Runlevel 6 = reboot      reboot.target
